steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to identify what causes meanders. The notes state the mechanism directly: water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits it on the opposite side. The correct choice must state this cause-and-effect relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Over time, many meanders have formed in the Madeira River, a river in Brazil with high sinuosity.: this describes a property of the Madeira River but says nothing about what causes meanders to form. ✗
  • Choice B — The meanders in the Madeira River will change shape and shift downstream over time.: this describes what meanders do after they form, not what causes them. ✗
  • Choice C — High sinuosity is caused by the formation of many meanders, or curves, in a riverbank over time.: this discusses sinuosity without explaining what causes meanders to form. ✗
  • Choice D — The erosion and redepositing of sediment from one side of a riverbank to the other forms meanders, or curves, over time.: this directly states the causal mechanism given in the notes. ✓
That is the correct answer.
